item.title: SAMDFuse: Semantic-Aware Multi-scale Degradation-robust Fusion network for SAR and optical images
item.dc:creator: Ruoxi Wang
Yinhe Li
Enhua Zhang
Wanni Chen
Kaizhi Wang
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Robust fusion of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) and optical imagery is critical for improving the performance of downstream tasks, such as land classification and target monitoring. However, existing fusion frameworks exhibit limited capability to suppress the inherent speckle noise in SAR images and lack effective mechanisms for handling degradation factors in optical images, such as cloud occlusion. Additionally, most current fusion algorithms prioritize visual enhancement, providing limited benefits for downstream tasks. To address these limitations, we propose a degradation-robust and semantic-aware SAR–optical image fusion network with three main contributions. First, an encoder architecture is designed with Sparse Feature Extraction Module (SFEM) layers. SFEM integrates Sparse Self-Attention (SSA) with a Hybrid-Scale Feed-forward Network (HSFN) to suppress speckle noise and enhance stable cross-modal discriminative features. Second, a degradation-aware data augmentation strategy is developed by simulating realistic cloud occlusions, encouraging the network to learn complementary cross-modal information and improving its generalization under complex conditions. Third, Semantic-Guided Fusion Modules (SGFMs) inject multi-scale semantic features into the decoder, enabling the fused results to maintain spectral and structural consistency while better supporting downstream semantic segmentation. Experiments on the WHU-OPT-SAR dataset demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches such as FusionMamba and VSFF, achieving a structural similarity index measure (SSIM) of 0.968, a visual information fidelity fusion (VIFF) of 0.498, and a mean intersection over union (mIoU) of 52.82%. Consistent improvements are also observed on additional datasets, while robust structural preservation and semantic consistency are maintained under severe degradations. The project is available at: https://github.com/christie6133/SAMDFuse . Published: 2026-05-09T13:07:28+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.846 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Ruoxi Wang; Yinhe Li; Enhua Zhang; Wanni Chen; Kaizhi Wang

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.04.049

Relevance: 0.846 (must_read)

Robust fusion of synthetic aperture radar (SAR) and optical imagery is critical for improving the performance of downstream tasks, such as land classification and target monitoring. However, existing fusion frameworks exhibit limited capability to suppress the inherent speckle noise in SAR images and lack effective mechanisms for handling degradation factors in optical images, such as cloud occlusion. Additionally, most current fusion algorithms prioritize visual enhancement, providing limited benefits for downstream tasks. To address these limitations, we propose a degradation-robust and semantic-aware SAR–optical image fusion network with three main contributions. First, an encoder architecture is designed with Sparse Feature Extraction Module (SFEM) layers. SFEM integrates Sparse Self-Attention (SSA) with a Hybrid-Scale Feed-forward Network (HSFN) to suppress speckle noise and enhance stable cross-modal discriminative features. Second, a degradation-aware data augmentation strategy is developed by simulating realistic cloud occlusions, encouraging the network to learn complementary cross-modal information and improving its generalization under complex conditions. Third, Semantic-Guided Fusion Modules (SGFMs) inject multi-scale semantic features into the decoder, enabling the fused results to maintain spectral and structural consistency while better supporting downstream semantic segmentation. Experiments on the WHU-OPT-SAR dataset demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches such as FusionMamba and VSFF, achieving a structural similarity index measure (SSIM) of 0.968, a visual information fidelity fusion (VIFF) of 0.498, and a mean intersection over union (mIoU) of 52.82%. Consistent improvements are also observed on additional datasets, while robust structural preservation and semantic consistency are maintained under severe degradations. The project is available at: https://github.com/christie6133/SAMDFuse .

item.title: DASFA-Net for Accurate Ship Detection in Optical Remote Sensing Images
item.dc:creator: Hanfu Li
Tianwei Zhang
Shikai Jiang
Xiyang Zhi
Dawei Wang
Lianru Gao
Dong Yang
Jindong Li
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Ship detection in optical remote sensing imagery is pivotal for various civilian and military applications. However, due to the presence of cluttered backgrounds and the significant variations in ship scales, accurately detecting ships in complex maritime environments remains challenging. To address these challenges, we propose the Dual Attention and Scale-aware Feature Alignment Network (DASFA-Net), to effectively detect multi-scale ship targets under clutter interference. The DASFA-Net comprises two primary components: Deformable Spatial Attention Module with Channel Integration (DSAM-CI) and Bidirectional Flow Alignment Network (BiFAN). The DSAM-CI enhances discrimination between ships and distractors by jointly modeling correlations across the channel and spatial domains and applying deformable spatial attention. Concurrently, BiFAN employs a bidirectional feature flow alignment strategy to resolve spatial misalignment in feature fusion, significantly improving the quality of multi-scale feature fusion. Lastly, we design the Dynamic Alpha Complete Intersection over Union (DA-CIoU) based on prior knowledge of target characteristics to explicitly refine our detector for maritime ships. Extensive experiments on three challenging ship detection benchmarks, including HRSC2016, SCCOS, and FGSRCS have demonstrated the superiority of our DASFA. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:47+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.836 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Hanfu Li; Tianwei Zhang; Shikai Jiang; Xiyang Zhi; Dawei Wang; Lianru Gao; Dong Yang; Jindong Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3691594

Relevance: 0.836 (must_read)

Ship detection in optical remote sensing imagery is pivotal for various civilian and military applications. However, due to the presence of cluttered backgrounds and the significant variations in ship scales, accurately detecting ships in complex maritime environments remains challenging. To address these challenges, we propose the Dual Attention and Scale-aware Feature Alignment Network (DASFA-Net), to effectively detect multi-scale ship targets under clutter interference. The DASFA-Net comprises two primary components: Deformable Spatial Attention Module with Channel Integration (DSAM-CI) and Bidirectional Flow Alignment Network (BiFAN). The DSAM-CI enhances discrimination between ships and distractors by jointly modeling correlations across the channel and spatial domains and applying deformable spatial attention. Concurrently, BiFAN employs a bidirectional feature flow alignment strategy to resolve spatial misalignment in feature fusion, significantly improving the quality of multi-scale feature fusion. Lastly, we design the Dynamic Alpha Complete Intersection over Union (DA-CIoU) based on prior knowledge of target characteristics to explicitly refine our detector for maritime ships. Extensive experiments on three challenging ship detection benchmarks, including HRSC2016, SCCOS, and FGSRCS have demonstrated the superiority of our DASFA.

item.title: Bidirectional Motion Perception Transformer Network for Multi-frame Infrared Small Target Detection
item.dc:creator: Zhiwei Jiang
Xiumei Wang
Xiangyang Li
Chunna Tian
Heng Zhou
Zhenxi Zhang
Shiguo Chen
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Multi-frame infrared small target detection (MIRSTD) aims to detect weak and fast-moving targets by leveraging temporal information across consecutive frames. However, existing MIRSTD methods primarily focus on intra-frame spatial contrast while insufficiently modeling temporal dependencies, leading to weak motion perception and poor temporal coherence. To address these issues, we propose BMPTransNet, a Bidirectional Motion Perception Transformer Network that effectively captures motion information across consecutive frames. First, a multi-scale encoder with a Spatial-embedded Channel Interaction Block (SCIB) is employed to generate fused spatial features, which are redistributed through a pyramid structure to improve the semantic distinction. Second, a Bidirectional Motion Perception Branch (BMPB) is introduced to align multi-frame features, extracting salient temporal cues while suppressing dynamic background interference. Third, a multi-frame joint iterative decoder augments initial queries with additional motion-guided queries derived from shallow features and temporal motion information. Finally, a self-supervised motion-consistency learning strategy guides the network to maintain temporal coherence and spatial accuracy across consecutive frames. Experimental results on three public datasets demonstrate that BMPTransNet achieves accurate detection of moving infrared small targets. Published: 2026-05-08T19:42:07+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.835 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zhiwei Jiang; Xiumei Wang; Xiangyang Li; Chunna Tian; Heng Zhou; Zhenxi Zhang; Shiguo Chen

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3691319

Relevance: 0.835 (must_read)

Multi-frame infrared small target detection (MIRSTD) aims to detect weak and fast-moving targets by leveraging temporal information across consecutive frames. However, existing MIRSTD methods primarily focus on intra-frame spatial contrast while insufficiently modeling temporal dependencies, leading to weak motion perception and poor temporal coherence. To address these issues, we propose BMPTransNet, a Bidirectional Motion Perception Transformer Network that effectively captures motion information across consecutive frames. First, a multi-scale encoder with a Spatial-embedded Channel Interaction Block (SCIB) is employed to generate fused spatial features, which are redistributed through a pyramid structure to improve the semantic distinction. Second, a Bidirectional Motion Perception Branch (BMPB) is introduced to align multi-frame features, extracting salient temporal cues while suppressing dynamic background interference. Third, a multi-frame joint iterative decoder augments initial queries with additional motion-guided queries derived from shallow features and temporal motion information. Finally, a self-supervised motion-consistency learning strategy guides the network to maintain temporal coherence and spatial accuracy across consecutive frames. Experimental results on three public datasets demonstrate that BMPTransNet achieves accurate detection of moving infrared small targets.

item.title: Generative Learning Imaging Framework for Millimeter Wave Synthetic Aperture Radar
item.dc:creator: Mou Wang
Yifei Hu
Hao Zhang
Xiang Cai
Jiahui Chen
Shunjun Wei
Jun Shi
Guolong Cui
Lingjiang Kong
Yongxin Guo
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
item.description: Accurately reconstructing synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images from incomplete echo measurements is the fundamental problem in simplifying SAR systems and reducing sensing costs. In solving this problem, the recently emerged deep learning SAR imaging concept shows high potential in offering both high accuracy and efficiency. However, such approaches face challenges in network topology designing, weights initialization, and dataset construction, which hinder their practical deployments. To address these problems, we propose a generative learning imaging framework, namely, generative learning imaging framework (GLIm), for millimeter wave (mmWave) SAR in tasks of sparsely sensing scenarios. In our scheme, the targeting images are generated from a specifically designed SAR image generator by mapping low-dimensional random noise to the scattering domain. In addition, the SAR image generator is trained based on a compound loss function where the incomplete echo measurements serve as the supervised signal. Unlike traditional algorithms, the algorithm proposed in this article solves the optimal solution purely through numerical propagation by online learning, avoiding the theoretical derivation of the iterative optimization process. Finally, experiments are carried out on both simulated and real-measured data, and both numerical and visual results demonstrate the viability of the proposed GLIm in the tasks of reconstructing mmWave SAR images from sparsely sampled echo measurements. Published: 2026-05-08T19:40:39+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems Score: 0.829 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mou Wang; Yifei Hu; Hao Zhang; Xiang Cai; Jiahui Chen; Shunjun Wei; Jun Shi; Guolong Cui; Lingjiang Kong; Yongxin Guo

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems

DOI: 10.1109/tnnls.2026.3686777

Relevance: 0.829 (must_read)

Accurately reconstructing synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images from incomplete echo measurements is the fundamental problem in simplifying SAR systems and reducing sensing costs. In solving this problem, the recently emerged deep learning SAR imaging concept shows high potential in offering both high accuracy and efficiency. However, such approaches face challenges in network topology designing, weights initialization, and dataset construction, which hinder their practical deployments. To address these problems, we propose a generative learning imaging framework, namely, generative learning imaging framework (GLIm), for millimeter wave (mmWave) SAR in tasks of sparsely sensing scenarios. In our scheme, the targeting images are generated from a specifically designed SAR image generator by mapping low-dimensional random noise to the scattering domain. In addition, the SAR image generator is trained based on a compound loss function where the incomplete echo measurements serve as the supervised signal. Unlike traditional algorithms, the algorithm proposed in this article solves the optimal solution purely through numerical propagation by online learning, avoiding the theoretical derivation of the iterative optimization process. Finally, experiments are carried out on both simulated and real-measured data, and both numerical and visual results demonstrate the viability of the proposed GLIm in the tasks of reconstructing mmWave SAR images from sparsely sampled echo measurements.

item.title: A Comprehensive Survey of Pretrained Models for Autonomous Driving: Methods and Applications
item.dc:creator: Renze Hu
Zhishuai Yin
Linzhen Nie
Yinghan Zhou
Hongchen Guo
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Pretrained models (PTMs), developed under the pretraining-to-tuning paradigm, have significantly advanced the capabilities of autonomous driving (AD) systems, demonstrating improved generalization, robustness, and engineering efficiency compared with models trained from scratch. In this paper, we provide a comprehensive and comparative survey of PTMs applied to AD over the past five years. Through an extensive review of existing literature, we first provide a detailed overview of PTMs from the perspectives of key pretraining strategies and tuning methods. Then, we propose a task-oriented taxonomy encompassing both modular and end-to-end driving systems, elucidating how PTMs are leveraged to address the challenges encountered across diverse driving tasks. In addition, we summarize representative benchmark datasets, simulation platforms, and open-source implementations to support systematic and reproducible research within the community. Finally, we discuss the remaining challenges associated with applying PTMs to AD and outline promising directions for future exploration. To ensure the continued relevance and timeliness of this study, we maintain a dynamic repository of related literature and resources, which is publicly accessible at PTM4AD . Published: 2026-05-08T12:53:52+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.818 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Renze Hu; Zhishuai Yin; Linzhen Nie; Yinghan Zhou; Hongchen Guo

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104457

Relevance: 0.818 (must_read)

Pretrained models (PTMs), developed under the pretraining-to-tuning paradigm, have significantly advanced the capabilities of autonomous driving (AD) systems, demonstrating improved generalization, robustness, and engineering efficiency compared with models trained from scratch. In this paper, we provide a comprehensive and comparative survey of PTMs applied to AD over the past five years. Through an extensive review of existing literature, we first provide a detailed overview of PTMs from the perspectives of key pretraining strategies and tuning methods. Then, we propose a task-oriented taxonomy encompassing both modular and end-to-end driving systems, elucidating how PTMs are leveraged to address the challenges encountered across diverse driving tasks. In addition, we summarize representative benchmark datasets, simulation platforms, and open-source implementations to support systematic and reproducible research within the community. Finally, we discuss the remaining challenges associated with applying PTMs to AD and outline promising directions for future exploration. To ensure the continued relevance and timeliness of this study, we maintain a dynamic repository of related literature and resources, which is publicly accessible at PTM4AD .

item.title: Na-IRSTD: Enhancing Infrared Small Target Detection via Native-Resolution Feature Selection and Fusion
item.dc:creator: Qian Xu
Chi Zhang
Qiming Zhang
Xi Li
Haojuan Yuan
Mingjin Zhang
item.prism:publicationName: arXiv
item.description: Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) faces the inherent challenge of precisely localizing dim targets amid complex background clutter. While progress has been made, existing methods usually follow conventional strategies to downsample features and discard small targets' details, resulting in suboptimal performance. In this paper, we present Na-IRSTD, a native-resolution feature extraction and fusion framework for IRSTD. This framework elegantly incorporates native-resolution features to preserve subtle target cues, overcoming the resolution limitations of existing infrared approaches and significantly improving the model's ability to localize small targets. We also introduce an effective token reduction and selection strategy, which selects target patches with high accuracy and confidence, boosting the low-level details of the feature while effectively reducing native-resolution patch tokens compared to dense processing, thereby avoiding imposing an unbearable computational burden. Extensive experiments demonstrate the robustness and effectiveness of our token reduction and selection strategy across multiple public datasets. Ultimately, our Na-IRSTD model achieves state-of-the-art performance on four benchmarks. Published: 2026-05-07T07:42:03+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.814 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Qian Xu; Chi Zhang; Qiming Zhang; Xi Li; Haojuan Yuan; Mingjin Zhang

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.814 (must_read)

Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) faces the inherent challenge of precisely localizing dim targets amid complex background clutter. While progress has been made, existing methods usually follow conventional strategies to downsample features and discard small targets' details, resulting in suboptimal performance. In this paper, we present Na-IRSTD, a native-resolution feature extraction and fusion framework for IRSTD. This framework elegantly incorporates native-resolution features to preserve subtle target cues, overcoming the resolution limitations of existing infrared approaches and significantly improving the model's ability to localize small targets. We also introduce an effective token reduction and selection strategy, which selects target patches with high accuracy and confidence, boosting the low-level details of the feature while effectively reducing native-resolution patch tokens compared to dense processing, thereby avoiding imposing an unbearable computational burden. Extensive experiments demonstrate the robustness and effectiveness of our token reduction and selection strategy across multiple public datasets. Ultimately, our Na-IRSTD model achieves state-of-the-art performance on four benchmarks.

item.title: EchoSR: Efficient Context Harnessing for Lightweight Image Super-Resolution
item.dc:creator: Hanli Zhao
Binhao Wang
Shihao Zhao
Tao Wang
Kaihao Zhang
Wanglong Lu
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Image super-resolution (SR) aims to reconstruct high-quality, high-resolution (HR) images from low-resolution (LR) inputs and plays a critical role in various downstream applications. Despite recent advancements, balancing reconstruction fidelity and computational efficiency remains a fundamental challenge, particularly in resource-constrained scenarios. While existing lightweight methods attempt to expand receptive fields, many of them either incur substantial computational overhead, naively scale up kernel sizes, or lack mechanisms for coherent multi-scale integration, limiting their overall effectiveness and scalability. To address these limitations, we propose EchoSR, an efficient context-harnessing framework for lightweight image super-resolution, which unifies multi-scale receptive field modeling and hierarchical context fusion. EchoSR decouples feature learning into disentangled local, multi-scale, and global modeling stages through an efficient context-harnessing strategy, and further promotes seamless cross-scale integration via a cross-scale overlapping fusion mechanism. Extensive experiments have shown that EchoSR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art lightweight super-resolution methods across multiple benchmarks, while also achieving a faster speed ( ∼ 2 × ). The source code is available at https://github.com/funnyWang-Echoes/EchoSR . Published: 2026-05-08T23:37:14+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.811 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Hanli Zhao; Binhao Wang; Shihao Zhao; Tao Wang; Kaihao Zhang; Wanglong Lu

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104471

Relevance: 0.811 (must_read)

Image super-resolution (SR) aims to reconstruct high-quality, high-resolution (HR) images from low-resolution (LR) inputs and plays a critical role in various downstream applications. Despite recent advancements, balancing reconstruction fidelity and computational efficiency remains a fundamental challenge, particularly in resource-constrained scenarios. While existing lightweight methods attempt to expand receptive fields, many of them either incur substantial computational overhead, naively scale up kernel sizes, or lack mechanisms for coherent multi-scale integration, limiting their overall effectiveness and scalability. To address these limitations, we propose EchoSR, an efficient context-harnessing framework for lightweight image super-resolution, which unifies multi-scale receptive field modeling and hierarchical context fusion. EchoSR decouples feature learning into disentangled local, multi-scale, and global modeling stages through an efficient context-harnessing strategy, and further promotes seamless cross-scale integration via a cross-scale overlapping fusion mechanism. Extensive experiments have shown that EchoSR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art lightweight super-resolution methods across multiple benchmarks, while also achieving a faster speed ( ∼ 2 × ). The source code is available at https://github.com/funnyWang-Echoes/EchoSR .

item.title: A Fine-Grained Semantic-Driven Pedestrian Identity-Aware Method for Visible and Infrared Image Fusion
item.dc:creator: Xiaowen Liu
Hongtao Huo
Haozhe Cao
Renhua Wang
Xu Dong
Shan Pang
Jing Li
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Visible and infrared image fusion (VIF) aims to integrate multi-modal complementary information for comprehensive scene representation, but existing vision-guided and semantic-driven VIF methods rely on coarse-grained consistency constraints. This causes modal shift and fine-grained semantic loss, severely degrading the performance of fused images in high-precision downstream tasks such as person re-identification (ReID), which requires accurate pedestrian identity features. To address this issue, we propose a unified optimization framework that integrates pedestrian identity perception and VIF, with fine-grained semantic constraints to learn a more discriminative cross-modal fusion space. First, we design a simulated pedestrian-scene synthesis method to generate training data with rich backgrounds and identity-annotated foregrounds by fusing mainstream ReID and VIF datasets. Second, we present DPSFusion, a fusion method based on a dual-stage progressive semantic refinement mechanism, which includes Feature-level Coarse Optimization (FCO) for initial cross-modal semantic alignment and Semantic-level Fine-tuning (SFT) for precise semantic refinement via cross-task supervision. Additionally, we construct the first identity-annotated VIF validation dataset to directly quantify the retention of identity-related fine-grained information in fusion results. Qualitative and quantitative experiments confirm that our method outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) approaches in visual presentation, and validation on object detection and ReID tasks verifies its superior ability to preserve both coarse-grained and fine-grained semantics. The code and data will be publicly released upon paper publication. Published: 2026-05-08T23:37:25+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.809 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiaowen Liu; Hongtao Huo; Haozhe Cao; Renhua Wang; Xu Dong; Shan Pang; Jing Li

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104453

Relevance: 0.809 (must_read)

Visible and infrared image fusion (VIF) aims to integrate multi-modal complementary information for comprehensive scene representation, but existing vision-guided and semantic-driven VIF methods rely on coarse-grained consistency constraints. This causes modal shift and fine-grained semantic loss, severely degrading the performance of fused images in high-precision downstream tasks such as person re-identification (ReID), which requires accurate pedestrian identity features. To address this issue, we propose a unified optimization framework that integrates pedestrian identity perception and VIF, with fine-grained semantic constraints to learn a more discriminative cross-modal fusion space. First, we design a simulated pedestrian-scene synthesis method to generate training data with rich backgrounds and identity-annotated foregrounds by fusing mainstream ReID and VIF datasets. Second, we present DPSFusion, a fusion method based on a dual-stage progressive semantic refinement mechanism, which includes Feature-level Coarse Optimization (FCO) for initial cross-modal semantic alignment and Semantic-level Fine-tuning (SFT) for precise semantic refinement via cross-task supervision. Additionally, we construct the first identity-annotated VIF validation dataset to directly quantify the retention of identity-related fine-grained information in fusion results. Qualitative and quantitative experiments confirm that our method outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) approaches in visual presentation, and validation on object detection and ReID tasks verifies its superior ability to preserve both coarse-grained and fine-grained semantics. The code and data will be publicly released upon paper publication.

item.title: Adaptive 3D Convolution for Remote Sensing Image Fusion
item.dc:creator: Siran Peng
Xiangyu Zhu
Shang-Qi Deng
Liang-Jian Deng
Zhen Lei
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Remote sensing image fusion aims to create a high-resolution multi/hyper-spectral image from a high-resolution image with limited spectral information and a low-resolution image with abundant spectral data. Recently, deep learning (DL) techniques have shown significant effectiveness in this area. Most DL-based methods approach image fusion as a 2D problem by encoding spectral information into feature map channels. However, our research suggests that this strategy introduces notable spectral distortions. In contrast, some methods consider spectral data as an additional dimension, utilizing standard 3D convolutions to preserve spectral information. Nevertheless, in a standard 3D convolutional layer, the same set of kernels is applied across all input regions, which we have found to be sub-optimal for image fusion. Furthermore, standard 3D convolutions necessitate substantial computational resources. To address these challenges, we propose a novel convolutional paradigm called Adaptive 3D Convolution (Ada3D) for remote sensing image fusion. Ada3D applies a unique set of 3D kernels to each input voxel, enabling the capture of fine-grained details. These adaptive kernels are generated through a two-step process: (i) spatial and spectral kernels are derived from their respective image sources; (ii) these two types of kernels are then combined to form content-aware 3D kernels that effectively integrate spatial and spectral information. Additionally, adaptive biases are introduced to enhance the convolutional outcome at the voxel level. Furthermore, we incorporate the group convolution technique to reduce computational complexity. As a result, Ada3D offers full adaptivity in an efficient manner. Evaluation results across five datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, underscoring the superiority of Ada3D. The code is available at https://github.com/PSRben/Ada3D. Published: 2026-05-08T19:43:05+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.807 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Siran Peng; Xiangyu Zhu; Shang-Qi Deng; Liang-Jian Deng; Zhen Lei

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3689418

Relevance: 0.807 (must_read)

Remote sensing image fusion aims to create a high-resolution multi/hyper-spectral image from a high-resolution image with limited spectral information and a low-resolution image with abundant spectral data. Recently, deep learning (DL) techniques have shown significant effectiveness in this area. Most DL-based methods approach image fusion as a 2D problem by encoding spectral information into feature map channels. However, our research suggests that this strategy introduces notable spectral distortions. In contrast, some methods consider spectral data as an additional dimension, utilizing standard 3D convolutions to preserve spectral information. Nevertheless, in a standard 3D convolutional layer, the same set of kernels is applied across all input regions, which we have found to be sub-optimal for image fusion. Furthermore, standard 3D convolutions necessitate substantial computational resources. To address these challenges, we propose a novel convolutional paradigm called Adaptive 3D Convolution (Ada3D) for remote sensing image fusion. Ada3D applies a unique set of 3D kernels to each input voxel, enabling the capture of fine-grained details. These adaptive kernels are generated through a two-step process: (i) spatial and spectral kernels are derived from their respective image sources; (ii) these two types of kernels are then combined to form content-aware 3D kernels that effectively integrate spatial and spectral information. Additionally, adaptive biases are introduced to enhance the convolutional outcome at the voxel level. Furthermore, we incorporate the group convolution technique to reduce computational complexity. As a result, Ada3D offers full adaptivity in an efficient manner. Evaluation results across five datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, underscoring the superiority of Ada3D. The code is available at https://github.com/PSRben/Ada3D.

item.title: D-PolTransGAN: Scattering Guided Hybrid Transformer-GAN for SAR-Optical Image Translation using Dual Polarization SAR
item.dc:creator: Jagrati Talreja
Tewodros Syum Gebre
Leila Hashemi-Beni
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) provides reliable imaging in all weather and lighting conditions, but its speckled appearance makes visual interpretation difficult and limits its integration with optical-based analyses. To address this challenge, we propose a new SAR-to-optical image translation framework tailored for flood assessment. The proposed model processes SAR VV and VH polarizations through separate feature extraction streams, enabling each stem to learn its unique scattering characteristics. These features are then fused using cross-attention to capture complementary information from both polarizations. The architecture combines convolutional networks for local detail extraction with transformer modules to model global context, all within a generative adversarial learning framework. We train and evaluate the model using the DEEPFLOOD, SEN1FLOODS11 and SEN12MS datasets, which provides well-aligned Sentinel-1 (VV, VH) and Sentinel-2 image pairs. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in terms of PSNR, SSIM, and perceptual quality (LPIPS), while preserving fine spatial details critical for flood analysis. Qualitative results on real flood events further demonstrate that the generated optical-like images recover visual structures and contextual information that are difficult to interpret in raw SAR imagery. https://github.com/JagratiTalreja01/D-PolTransGAN Published: 2026-05-08T19:40:24+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.806 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jagrati Talreja; Tewodros Syum Gebre; Leila Hashemi-Beni

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3691677

Relevance: 0.806 (must_read)

Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) provides reliable imaging in all weather and lighting conditions, but its speckled appearance makes visual interpretation difficult and limits its integration with optical-based analyses. To address this challenge, we propose a new SAR-to-optical image translation framework tailored for flood assessment. The proposed model processes SAR VV and VH polarizations through separate feature extraction streams, enabling each stem to learn its unique scattering characteristics. These features are then fused using cross-attention to capture complementary information from both polarizations. The architecture combines convolutional networks for local detail extraction with transformer modules to model global context, all within a generative adversarial learning framework. We train and evaluate the model using the DEEPFLOOD, SEN1FLOODS11 and SEN12MS datasets, which provides well-aligned Sentinel-1 (VV, VH) and Sentinel-2 image pairs. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in terms of PSNR, SSIM, and perceptual quality (LPIPS), while preserving fine spatial details critical for flood analysis. Qualitative results on real flood events further demonstrate that the generated optical-like images recover visual structures and contextual information that are difficult to interpret in raw SAR imagery. https://github.com/JagratiTalreja01/D-PolTransGAN

item.title: Multimodal-Interactive GCN-Guided Space-Frequency Implicit Fusion Network for Semantic Segmentation of Optical and SAR Images
item.dc:creator: Bingxia Xin
Hongqing Li
Chunyu Zhu
Rongyuan Dai
Qiong Wu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Multi-source remote sensing images capture complementary information of ground objects and provide valuable inputs for semantic segmentation, and different sensors may produce modality inconsistencies and interference noise. Optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) segmentation is challenging for three main reasons. First, geometric distortions introduce scale inconsistencies. Second, speckle noise degrades signal quality. Third, subtle inter-class differences lead to misalignment between modalities. Collectively, these factors aggravate cross-modal contextual inconsistency and hinder accurate multimodal segmentation. We propose MSFNet, a graph-guided spatial-frequency implicit fusion network that unifies graph priors with continuous implicit representations. A dual-path encoder processes optical images with a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and SAR with a Dynamic Adaptive Graph Convolutional Network(DA-GCN) to capture topological/scattering structures. Fusion is performed by Graph-guided Spatial Implicit Sampling (GSIS) for geometry-aware alignment in the continuous coordinate space and Graph-guided Fourier Implicit Sampling(GFIS) for frequency-aware detail compensation and global context modulation. A multi-scale decoder yields fine-grained boundaries. On WHU-OPT-SAR, DFC23, YYX-OPT-SAR and DFC2025, MSFNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, with consistent gains in Boundary F1-score (Boundary-F1) and boundary intersection-over-union (bIoU), as well as robustness to misregistration and speckle. These results demonstrate that structure-aware graph modeling and spatial-frequency implicit fusion jointly improve accuracy and robustness for multimodal remote-sensing segmentation. The code will be shared on GitHub soon. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:47+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.805 (must_read)

Authors: Bingxia Xin; Hongqing Li; Chunyu Zhu; Rongyuan Dai; Qiong Wu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3691414

Relevance: 0.805 (must_read)

Multi-source remote sensing images capture complementary information of ground objects and provide valuable inputs for semantic segmentation, and different sensors may produce modality inconsistencies and interference noise. Optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) segmentation is challenging for three main reasons. First, geometric distortions introduce scale inconsistencies. Second, speckle noise degrades signal quality. Third, subtle inter-class differences lead to misalignment between modalities. Collectively, these factors aggravate cross-modal contextual inconsistency and hinder accurate multimodal segmentation. We propose MSFNet, a graph-guided spatial-frequency implicit fusion network that unifies graph priors with continuous implicit representations. A dual-path encoder processes optical images with a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and SAR with a Dynamic Adaptive Graph Convolutional Network(DA-GCN) to capture topological/scattering structures. Fusion is performed by Graph-guided Spatial Implicit Sampling (GSIS) for geometry-aware alignment in the continuous coordinate space and Graph-guided Fourier Implicit Sampling(GFIS) for frequency-aware detail compensation and global context modulation. A multi-scale decoder yields fine-grained boundaries. On WHU-OPT-SAR, DFC23, YYX-OPT-SAR and DFC2025, MSFNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, with consistent gains in Boundary F1-score (Boundary-F1) and boundary intersection-over-union (bIoU), as well as robustness to misregistration and speckle. These results demonstrate that structure-aware graph modeling and spatial-frequency implicit fusion jointly improve accuracy and robustness for multimodal remote-sensing segmentation. The code will be shared on GitHub soon.

item.title: StratiFormer: Stratified Temporal Transformer for video object detection
item.dc:creator: Wentao Zheng
Hong Zheng
Yuquan Sun
Ying Jing
item.prism:publicationName: Knowledge-Based Systems
item.description: In video object detection (VOD), exploiting temporal cues is essential for alleviating common visual degradations (e.g., motion blur and occlusion). Existing approaches range from short-range feature enhancement to long-range dependency aggregation, while some attempt to combine both. Although substantial progress has been achieved, most designs still decouple temporal aggregation from multi-scale feature construction. In particular, many methods adopt a unified temporal aggregation range across all feature levels, which is level-agnostic and overlooks the distinct temporal requirements of low-level features for fine-grained localization and high-level features for abstract semantics. This limitation restricts a model’s ability to adaptively utilize temporal information according to the feature hierarchy. To address this issue, we propose the Stratified Temporal Transformer (StratiFormer). StratiFormer integrates the fusion of long- and short-range temporal cues directly into the cross-scale feature construction process, enabling feature-level-adaptive temporal aggregation. Moreover, it employs a stratified selection mechanism to identify and process a sparse yet informative set of temporal features at each feature level, allowing the model to allocate computation to the most relevant candidates. On the ImageNet VID benchmark, StratiFormer achieves up to 88.2% mAP 50 " role="presentation"> mAP 50 mAP 50 , delivering a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency. Published: 2026-05-09T15:15:25+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.803 (must_read)

Authors: Wentao Zheng; Hong Zheng; Yuquan Sun; Ying Jing

Journal: Knowledge-Based Systems

DOI: 10.1016/j.knosys.2026.116117

Relevance: 0.803 (must_read)

In video object detection (VOD), exploiting temporal cues is essential for alleviating common visual degradations (e.g., motion blur and occlusion). Existing approaches range from short-range feature enhancement to long-range dependency aggregation, while some attempt to combine both. Although substantial progress has been achieved, most designs still decouple temporal aggregation from multi-scale feature construction. In particular, many methods adopt a unified temporal aggregation range across all feature levels, which is level-agnostic and overlooks the distinct temporal requirements of low-level features for fine-grained localization and high-level features for abstract semantics. This limitation restricts a model’s ability to adaptively utilize temporal information according to the feature hierarchy. To address this issue, we propose the Stratified Temporal Transformer (StratiFormer). StratiFormer integrates the fusion of long- and short-range temporal cues directly into the cross-scale feature construction process, enabling feature-level-adaptive temporal aggregation. Moreover, it employs a stratified selection mechanism to identify and process a sparse yet informative set of temporal features at each feature level, allowing the model to allocate computation to the most relevant candidates. On the ImageNet VID benchmark, StratiFormer achieves up to 88.2% mAP 50 " role="presentation"> mAP 50 mAP 50 , delivering a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency.

item.title: Cloud-Type Classification using Vision-Language Model with Cloud Multi-Modal Adapter
item.dc:creator: Shuang Liu
Yuzhu Chu
Zhong Zhang
Baihua Xiao
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: The existing methods for cloud-type classification primarily rely on cloud observation data containing low-level semantic information, e.g., cloud images and millimeter-wave cloud radar (MMCR) observations, which limits the ability to bridge the gap between original cloud data and human cognitive understanding. In this paper, we apply Large Language Model (LLM) to generate cloud description texts which include high-level semantic information, and we integrate cloud images, MMCR observations and cloud description texts for cloud-type classification. To effectively fuse the information across modalities, we propose a novel adapter named Cloud Multi-Modal Adapter (CMMA) for Vision Language Model (VLM) fine-tuning. The proposed CMMA could adaptively fuse the image features with both radar and text features extracted from corresponding adapters, which enhances the fusion across different modalities and improves the model’s ability to learn from multi-modal data. Meanwhile, we propose the Tri-Modalities Contrastive Loss (TCL) to enhance cross-modal alignment while preserving intra-modal consistency. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:47+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.800 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Shuang Liu; Yuzhu Chu; Zhong Zhang; Baihua Xiao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3691532

Relevance: 0.800 (must_read)

The existing methods for cloud-type classification primarily rely on cloud observation data containing low-level semantic information, e.g., cloud images and millimeter-wave cloud radar (MMCR) observations, which limits the ability to bridge the gap between original cloud data and human cognitive understanding. In this paper, we apply Large Language Model (LLM) to generate cloud description texts which include high-level semantic information, and we integrate cloud images, MMCR observations and cloud description texts for cloud-type classification. To effectively fuse the information across modalities, we propose a novel adapter named Cloud Multi-Modal Adapter (CMMA) for Vision Language Model (VLM) fine-tuning. The proposed CMMA could adaptively fuse the image features with both radar and text features extracted from corresponding adapters, which enhances the fusion across different modalities and improves the model’s ability to learn from multi-modal data. Meanwhile, we propose the Tri-Modalities Contrastive Loss (TCL) to enhance cross-modal alignment while preserving intra-modal consistency. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach.

item.title: Efficient spectral-temporal reconstruction of long-term satellite time series via temporal segments and mask-informed embedding
item.dc:creator: Jie Yang
Xin Huang
item.prism:publicationName: Remote Sensing of Environment
item.description: Long-term satellite time series (STS) provide invaluable Earth observations for monitoring global change. However, they suffer from a high proportion of missing values due to cloud cover, shadows, and sensor limitations. Reconstructing these incomplete datasets to create a continuous spectral-temporal record is therefore crucial for maximizing their scientific value. While existing Transformer-based architectures show promise, they still face challenges in maintaining spectral consistency and ensuring robustness against complex missingness. In this paper, we propose mask-informed spectral-temporal Transformer (MISTR), a novel framework that addresses these challenges by first partitioning the original multi-spectral sequence into a set of parallel, single-band temporal segments. Building on this, we propose the mask-informed embedding (MIE) module, which generates feature embeddings that are robust to the underlying missingness, thereby explicitly enhancing the ability to represent incomplete data. The resulting stable embeddings are then processed by the temporal-spectral adaptive (TSA) blocks, which model temporal dynamics across time and spectral dependencies across bands, while implementing adaptive information flow control through a gate mechanism. We conducted experiments on 30 Landsat ARD tiles over China, spanning from 1997 to 2023. The results show that MISTR achieves competitive reconstruction accuracy, with a modest but consistent improvement over the strongest baseline when applied to multi-year satellite time series, while yielding better computational efficiency. Beyond pointwise reconstruction, MISTR learns more stable spectral–temporal representations that transfer effectively to downstream tasks, including land cover classification, change detection, and vegetation phenology. In addition, MISTR demonstrates cross-sensor flexibility for adaptation to Sentinel-2 and MODIS, and we further present a preliminary joint-sensor setting for collaborative reconstruction under sparse conditions. In summary, these results highlight the potential of MISTR for applications in the field of long-term STS analysis. Published: 2026-05-09T15:50:08+00:00 Venue: Remote Sensing of Environment Score: 0.800 (must_read)

Long-term satellite time series (STS) provide invaluable Earth observations for monitoring global change. However, they suffer from a high proportion of missing values due to cloud cover, shadows, and sensor limitations. Reconstructing these incomplete datasets to create a continuous spectral-temporal record is therefore crucial for maximizing their scientific value. While existing Transformer-based architectures show promise, they still face challenges in maintaining spectral consistency and ensuring robustness against complex missingness. In this paper, we propose mask-informed spectral-temporal Transformer (MISTR), a novel framework that addresses these challenges by first partitioning the original multi-spectral sequence into a set of parallel, single-band temporal segments. Building on this, we propose the mask-informed embedding (MIE) module, which generates feature embeddings that are robust to the underlying missingness, thereby explicitly enhancing the ability to represent incomplete data. The resulting stable embeddings are then processed by the temporal-spectral adaptive (TSA) blocks, which model temporal dynamics across time and spectral dependencies across bands, while implementing adaptive information flow control through a gate mechanism. We conducted experiments on 30 Landsat ARD tiles over China, spanning from 1997 to 2023. The results show that MISTR achieves competitive reconstruction accuracy, with a modest but consistent improvement over the strongest baseline when applied to multi-year satellite time series, while yielding better computational efficiency. Beyond pointwise reconstruction, MISTR learns more stable spectral–temporal representations that transfer effectively to downstream tasks, including land cover classification, change detection, and vegetation phenology. In addition, MISTR demonstrates cross-sensor flexibility for adaptation to Sentinel-2 and MODIS, and we further present a preliminary joint-sensor setting for collaborative reconstruction under sparse conditions. In summary, these results highlight the potential of MISTR for applications in the field of long-term STS analysis.

item.title: Interpretable Semantic Medical Image Segmentation with Style and Confidence
item.dc:creator: Wei Dai
Siyu Liu
Jurgen Fripp
Craig Engstrom
Shekhar S. Chandra
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: The scarcity of semantically labelled data presents major challenges for medical image segmentation using deep learning models, and the ”black-box” nature of these models inherently limits their interpretability during clinical deployment. To address these issues, we propose Generative Adaptable Segmentation Evolution (GASE), an end-to-end, style-based generative adversarial framework for robust and interpretable medical image segmentation under extreme data scarcity. Trained on limited single-sequence Magnetic Resonance (MR) data, GASE self-adapts to unseen acquisition-level image variations-arising from differences in scanning protocols, imaging sequences, and patient demographics-while simultaneously improving interpretability through automatic input validity assessment and output reliability estimation. During adversarial training, the style-learning generator captures a manifold-like representation of input style features for valid input interpretation and diversifies labelled training pairs through style interpolation to simulate acquisition-level image variations. The segmentation-based discriminator, trained on these diversified samples, further improves adaptation to unseen styles and automatically estimates the reliability of predicted masks via confidence learning. Extensive experiments on knee and pelvis MR image datasets demonstrate that GASE achieves high segmentation accuracy for assessments of multiple tissues under examination, strong adaptability to unseen acquisition-level image variations, and intrinsic interpretability, underscoring its potential for reliable clinical deployment. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:44+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.799 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Wei Dai; Siyu Liu; Jurgen Fripp; Craig Engstrom; Shekhar S. Chandra

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3689564

Relevance: 0.799 (must_read)

The scarcity of semantically labelled data presents major challenges for medical image segmentation using deep learning models, and the ”black-box” nature of these models inherently limits their interpretability during clinical deployment. To address these issues, we propose Generative Adaptable Segmentation Evolution (GASE), an end-to-end, style-based generative adversarial framework for robust and interpretable medical image segmentation under extreme data scarcity. Trained on limited single-sequence Magnetic Resonance (MR) data, GASE self-adapts to unseen acquisition-level image variations-arising from differences in scanning protocols, imaging sequences, and patient demographics-while simultaneously improving interpretability through automatic input validity assessment and output reliability estimation. During adversarial training, the style-learning generator captures a manifold-like representation of input style features for valid input interpretation and diversifies labelled training pairs through style interpolation to simulate acquisition-level image variations. The segmentation-based discriminator, trained on these diversified samples, further improves adaptation to unseen styles and automatically estimates the reliability of predicted masks via confidence learning. Extensive experiments on knee and pelvis MR image datasets demonstrate that GASE achieves high segmentation accuracy for assessments of multiple tissues under examination, strong adaptability to unseen acquisition-level image variations, and intrinsic interpretability, underscoring its potential for reliable clinical deployment.

item.title: Scalable Decomposition Gradients for Multimodal Learning
item.dc:creator: Mengxuan Wang
Hongjie Jiang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Optimizing multimodal models using unimodal objectives has proven effective in mitigating modality bias and enhancing generalization. This multitask-style training paradigm has motivated a range of gradient-based optimization methods. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on adjusting gradient magnitudes during aggregation, while overlooking the directional inconsistency between the final update direction and the gradients derived from unimodal and multimodal objectives, which may result in suboptimal optimization. To address this issue, we propose Scalable Decomposition Gradients (SDG), a novel framework for conflict-free optimization in multimodal learning. SDG explicitly decouples the optimization of gradient magnitude and direction. Specifically, we employ modality-aware orthogonal projections to remove conflicting components across gradients, ensuring that the final update direction aligns with both unimodal and multimodal learning objectives. We further utilize the projection residuals to adaptively scale each gradient, balancing their relative contributions and mitigating magnitude disparities. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that such conflict-free updates with consistent step sizes promote balanced convergence across losses from different modalities. Extensive experiments on several widely used multimodal benchmarks validate the effectiveness of SDG, showing notable performance improvements without introducing additional model complexity. Published: 2026-05-08T19:42:07+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.798 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mengxuan Wang; Hongjie Jiang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3691409

Relevance: 0.798 (must_read)

Optimizing multimodal models using unimodal objectives has proven effective in mitigating modality bias and enhancing generalization. This multitask-style training paradigm has motivated a range of gradient-based optimization methods. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on adjusting gradient magnitudes during aggregation, while overlooking the directional inconsistency between the final update direction and the gradients derived from unimodal and multimodal objectives, which may result in suboptimal optimization. To address this issue, we propose Scalable Decomposition Gradients (SDG), a novel framework for conflict-free optimization in multimodal learning. SDG explicitly decouples the optimization of gradient magnitude and direction. Specifically, we employ modality-aware orthogonal projections to remove conflicting components across gradients, ensuring that the final update direction aligns with both unimodal and multimodal learning objectives. We further utilize the projection residuals to adaptively scale each gradient, balancing their relative contributions and mitigating magnitude disparities. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that such conflict-free updates with consistent step sizes promote balanced convergence across losses from different modalities. Extensive experiments on several widely used multimodal benchmarks validate the effectiveness of SDG, showing notable performance improvements without introducing additional model complexity.

item.title: HYOLO: A Lightweight and Efficient Detection Framework for Small Objects in Aerial Images
item.dc:creator: Shixiao Wu
Huafeng Kong
Zhiying Xu
item.prism:publicationName: Expert Systems with Applications
item.description: Detecting small objects in UAV and remote sensing imagery remains a persistent challenge due to extremely limited pixel coverage, sparse feature representation, frequent occlusion, and complex backgrounds. Existing methods suffer from three common shortcomings in small-object detection: (1) conventional feature extraction paradigms struggle to capture discriminative features from cluttered backgrounds, leading to the loss of critical small-object features; (2) multi-scale fusion networks in existing lightweight models exhibit insufficient cross-level interaction and representation capacity; and (3) direct detection after fusion often neglects spatially critical regions, substantially degrading small-object accuracy. To address these issues, this paper proposes HYOLO, a novel framework for efficient and accurate small-object detection in complex aerial scenes. The framework introduces three core innovations: (1) a heterogeneous Multi-branch Dynamic Feature Fusion Module (MBDF) that combines complementary feature extraction pathways (micro-object, depthwise separable, and directionally asymmetric convolutions) via dual-attention fusion and cross-stage reuse; (2) a Recursive Attention Feature Pyramid Network (RAFPN) that establishes a closed-loop recursive architecture through three cascaded PANet modules with integrated attention refinement, enabling iterative enhancement of multi-scale feature representations while preserving critical spatial details; and (3) a Multi-scale Attentive Refinement Module for Small Objects (MARS) that employs stabilized dual-attention, Gaussian-based detail extraction, and parameter-controlled fusion. Evaluated on four challenging aerial benchmarks (VisDrone 2019, DIOR, UCAS-AOD, USOD), HYOLO achieves state-of-the-art or highly competitive accuracy (e.g., 51.1% mAP50 on VisDrone, 97.9% on UCAS-AOD, 92.2% on USOD) while requiring only 9.3M parameters and 103.8 GFLOPs. Notably, HYOLO attains superior performance with 2–5 × fewer parameters and 1.5–3 × lower computational costs than comparable state-of-the-art models. This efficiency–performance trade-off positions HYOLO as a practical solution for resource-constrained aerial applications. Published: 2026-05-09T23:10:58+00:00 Venue: Expert Systems with Applications Score: 0.796 (must_read)

Authors: Shixiao Wu; Huafeng Kong; Zhiying Xu

Journal: Expert Systems with Applications

DOI: 10.1016/j.eswa.2026.132794

Relevance: 0.796 (must_read)

Detecting small objects in UAV and remote sensing imagery remains a persistent challenge due to extremely limited pixel coverage, sparse feature representation, frequent occlusion, and complex backgrounds. Existing methods suffer from three common shortcomings in small-object detection: (1) conventional feature extraction paradigms struggle to capture discriminative features from cluttered backgrounds, leading to the loss of critical small-object features; (2) multi-scale fusion networks in existing lightweight models exhibit insufficient cross-level interaction and representation capacity; and (3) direct detection after fusion often neglects spatially critical regions, substantially degrading small-object accuracy. To address these issues, this paper proposes HYOLO, a novel framework for efficient and accurate small-object detection in complex aerial scenes. The framework introduces three core innovations: (1) a heterogeneous Multi-branch Dynamic Feature Fusion Module (MBDF) that combines complementary feature extraction pathways (micro-object, depthwise separable, and directionally asymmetric convolutions) via dual-attention fusion and cross-stage reuse; (2) a Recursive Attention Feature Pyramid Network (RAFPN) that establishes a closed-loop recursive architecture through three cascaded PANet modules with integrated attention refinement, enabling iterative enhancement of multi-scale feature representations while preserving critical spatial details; and (3) a Multi-scale Attentive Refinement Module for Small Objects (MARS) that employs stabilized dual-attention, Gaussian-based detail extraction, and parameter-controlled fusion. Evaluated on four challenging aerial benchmarks (VisDrone 2019, DIOR, UCAS-AOD, USOD), HYOLO achieves state-of-the-art or highly competitive accuracy (e.g., 51.1% mAP50 on VisDrone, 97.9% on UCAS-AOD, 92.2% on USOD) while requiring only 9.3M parameters and 103.8 GFLOPs. Notably, HYOLO attains superior performance with 2–5 × fewer parameters and 1.5–3 × lower computational costs than comparable state-of-the-art models. This efficiency–performance trade-off positions HYOLO as a practical solution for resource-constrained aerial applications.

item.title: From Zero to Detail: A Progressive Spectral Decoupling Paradigm for UHD Image Restoration with New Benchmark
item.dc:creator: Chen Zhao
Yunzhe Xu
Zhizhou Chen
Enxuan Gu
Kai Zhang
Xiaoming Liu
Jian Yang
Ying Tai
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Ultra-high-definition (UHD) image restoration poses unique challenges due to the high spatial resolution, diverse content, and fine-grained structures present in UHD images. To address these issues, we introduce a progressive spectral decomposition for the restoration process, decomposing it into three stages: zero-frequency enhancement, low-frequency restoration, and high-frequency refinement. Based on this formulation, we propose a novel framework, ERR, which integrates three cooperative sub-networks: the zero-frequency enhancer (ZFE), the low-frequency restorer (LFR), and the high-frequency refiner (HFR). The ZFE incorporates global priors to learn holistic mappings, the LFR reconstructs the main content by focusing on coarse-scale information, and the HFR adopts our proposed frequency-windowed Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (FW-KAN) to recover fine textures and intricate details for high-fidelity restoration. To further advance research in UHD image restoration, we also construct a large-scale, high-quality benchmark dataset, LSUHDIR, comprising 82,126 UHD images with diverse scenes and rich content. Our proposed methods demonstrate superior performance across a range of UHD image restoration tasks, and extensive ablation studies confirm the contribution and necessity of each module. Project page: https://github.com/NJU-PCALab/ERR. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:44+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.791 (must_read)

Ultra-high-definition (UHD) image restoration poses unique challenges due to the high spatial resolution, diverse content, and fine-grained structures present in UHD images. To address these issues, we introduce a progressive spectral decomposition for the restoration process, decomposing it into three stages: zero-frequency enhancement, low-frequency restoration, and high-frequency refinement. Based on this formulation, we propose a novel framework, ERR, which integrates three cooperative sub-networks: the zero-frequency enhancer (ZFE), the low-frequency restorer (LFR), and the high-frequency refiner (HFR). The ZFE incorporates global priors to learn holistic mappings, the LFR reconstructs the main content by focusing on coarse-scale information, and the HFR adopts our proposed frequency-windowed Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (FW-KAN) to recover fine textures and intricate details for high-fidelity restoration. To further advance research in UHD image restoration, we also construct a large-scale, high-quality benchmark dataset, LSUHDIR, comprising 82,126 UHD images with diverse scenes and rich content. Our proposed methods demonstrate superior performance across a range of UHD image restoration tasks, and extensive ablation studies confirm the contribution and necessity of each module. Project page: https://github.com/NJU-PCALab/ERR.

item.title: Reference-based Category Discovery: Unsupervised Object Detection with Category Awareness
item.dc:creator: Yichen Li
Qiankun Liu
Ying Fu
item.prism:publicationName: arXiv
item.description: Traditional one-shot detection methods have addressed the closed-set problem in object detection, but the high cost of data annotation remains a critical challenge. General unsupervised methods generate pseudo boxes without category labels, thus failing to achieve category-aware classification. To overcome these limitations, we propose Reference-based Category Discovery (RefCD), an unsupervised detector that enables category-aware\footnotemark[1] detection without any manually annotated labels. It leverages feature similarity between predicted objects and unlabeled reference images. Unlike previous unsupervised methods that lack category guidance and one-shot methods which require labeled data, RefCD introduces a carefully designed feature similarity loss to explicitly guide the learning of potential category-specific features. Additionally, RefCD supports category-agnostic detection without reference images, serving as a unified framework. Comprehensive quantitative and qualitative analysis of category-aware and category-agnostic detection results demonstrates its effectiveness, and RefCD can learn category information in an unsupervised paradigm even without category labels. Published: 2026-05-06T07:54:30+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.790 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yichen Li; Qiankun Liu; Ying Fu

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.790 (must_read)

Traditional one-shot detection methods have addressed the closed-set problem in object detection, but the high cost of data annotation remains a critical challenge. General unsupervised methods generate pseudo boxes without category labels, thus failing to achieve category-aware classification. To overcome these limitations, we propose Reference-based Category Discovery (RefCD), an unsupervised detector that enables category-aware\footnotemark[1] detection without any manually annotated labels. It leverages feature similarity between predicted objects and unlabeled reference images. Unlike previous unsupervised methods that lack category guidance and one-shot methods which require labeled data, RefCD introduces a carefully designed feature similarity loss to explicitly guide the learning of potential category-specific features. Additionally, RefCD supports category-agnostic detection without reference images, serving as a unified framework. Comprehensive quantitative and qualitative analysis of category-aware and category-agnostic detection results demonstrates its effectiveness, and RefCD can learn category information in an unsupervised paradigm even without category labels.

item.title: Local Cross-Channel Interaction Coverage Network for Infrared Small Target Detection
item.dc:creator: Yifan Ji
Ende Wang
Jiageng Liu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
item.description: Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) faces challenges such as low signal-to-noise ratios (SNR), complex backgrounds, and semantic information loss in downsampling, limiting robustness and efficiency of existing methods. To address this issue, a novel model, namely Local Cross-Channel Interaction Coverage Network (LCICNet), is proposed for infrared small target detection by integrating the Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) module with channel-adaptive kernels. Specially, we first introduce lightweight convolutions with adaptive kernels to improve computational efficiency, for which a local contrast method that expands the receptive field and minimizes the loss of high-level semantic information is given. Then, we design a Local Contrast Sensitivity (LCS) loss function, which adjusts Intersection over Union (IoU) loss weights based on target scale. Experimental results validate the effectiveness of LCICNet in addressing challenges in infrared small target detection, achieving accurate infrared small target detection with 0.43M parameters. The source can be found online at https://github.com/Jiyifan15/ LCICNet-for-IRSTD. Published: 2026-05-08T19:43:30+00:00 Venue: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters Score: 0.785 (must_read)

Authors: Yifan Ji; Ende Wang; Jiageng Liu

Journal: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters

DOI: 10.1109/lgrs.2026.3691332

Relevance: 0.785 (must_read)

Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) faces challenges such as low signal-to-noise ratios (SNR), complex backgrounds, and semantic information loss in downsampling, limiting robustness and efficiency of existing methods. To address this issue, a novel model, namely Local Cross-Channel Interaction Coverage Network (LCICNet), is proposed for infrared small target detection by integrating the Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) module with channel-adaptive kernels. Specially, we first introduce lightweight convolutions with adaptive kernels to improve computational efficiency, for which a local contrast method that expands the receptive field and minimizes the loss of high-level semantic information is given. Then, we design a Local Contrast Sensitivity (LCS) loss function, which adjusts Intersection over Union (IoU) loss weights based on target scale. Experimental results validate the effectiveness of LCICNet in addressing challenges in infrared small target detection, achieving accurate infrared small target detection with 0.43M parameters. The source can be found online at https://github.com/Jiyifan15/ LCICNet-for-IRSTD.

item.title: MVHumanNet++: A Large-scale Dataset of Multi-view Daily Dressing Human Captures with Richer Annotations for 3D Human Digitization
item.dc:creator: Chenghong Li
Hongjie Liao
Yihao Zhi
Xihe Yang
Zhengwentai Sun
Jiahao Chang
Shuguang Cui
Xiaoguang Han
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: In this era, the success of large language models and text-to-image models can be attributed to the driving force of large-scale datasets. However, in the realm of 3D vision, while significant progress has been achieved in object-centric tasks through large-scale datasets like Objaverse and MVImgNet, human-centric tasks have seen limited advancement, largely due to the absence of a comparable large-scale human dataset. To bridge this gap, we present MVHumanNet++, a dataset that comprises multi-view human action sequences of 4,500 human identities. The primary focus of our work is on collecting human data that features a large number of diverse identities and everyday clothing using multi-view human capture systems, which facilitates easily scalable data collection. Our dataset contains 9,000 daily outfits, 60,000 motion sequences and 645 million frames with extensive annotations, including human masks, camera parameters, 2D and 3D keypoints, SMPL/SMPLX parameters, and corresponding textual descriptions. Additionally, the proposed MVHumanNet++ dataset is enhanced with newly processed normal maps and depth maps, significantly expanding its applicability and utility for advanced human-centric research. To explore the potential of our proposed MVHumanNet++ dataset in various 2D and 3D visual tasks, we conducted several pilot studies to demonstrate the performance improvements and effective applications enabled by the scale provided by MVHumanNet++. As the current largest-scale 3D human dataset, we hope that the release of MVHumanNet++ dataset with annotations will foster further innovations in the domain of 3D human-centric tasks at scale. MVHumanNet++ is publicly available at https://kevinlee09.github.io/research/MVHumanNet++/. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:44+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.783 (must_read)
